title: Watching From the Stands
prose: Missing out on a team spot doesn't mean missing out on Quidditch — if anything, the first big match of the season from the stands is its own kind of thrilling, all scarves and rattling noise-makers and the whole crowd lurching sideways as one when a Bludger goes wide.
You lose your voice somewhere in the second half, cheering for a team you didn't make. It doesn't feel like much of a loss.

choices.label: Even from the stands, it's brilliant.
Watching only sharpens the itch to actually play.
